step_by_step_thinking:
I concur with selecting Option C – Thorough Clinical Assessment. Although fluoride mouthwash plays a crucial role in reducing new carious lesions, its efficacy lies primarily in prophylaxis rather than active disease surveillance. It complements the clinical examination process but doesn't serve as a replacement for it. Routine clinical evaluations remain vital because they facilitate the early detection, documentation, and management of existing cavities, ensuring prompt intervention if necessary. Additionally, clinical assessments can guide personalized treatment planning and education regarding homecare practices specifically suited to the unique needs of preschool-aged children.
